gauge.name: Meramec River near Eureka

impacts.statement: The Raineri Construction Products building on Truitt Drive begins flooding near this height.
The Shell station between south Central Avenue and Highway 109 building and the Eureka Post Office building will both begin flooding near this height.
Midwest Motors dealership's office building begins flooding at this height.
Gershenson Construction on Truitt Drive begins flooding at this height.
The Legion Park Community Center will begin flooding near this height.
Three businesses along Truitt Drive, Earthbound Recycling Center, J.M. Mersheutz Construction, and Sellenriek Grading, begin flooding near this height.
The intersection of Highway 109 with highways W and FF will begin flooding near this height.
Cotton's Ace Hardware between South Central Avenue and Highway 109 begins flooding near this height.
Near this height, floodwaters will reach the top of the rim around the wastewater treatment plant lagoon.
Near this height, numerous businesses along Central Avenue in old town Eureka, Rockwood Bank, and the sewage treatment plant will begin flooding.
Missouri State Route 109 in the older business section of Eureka is closed near this height.
Missouri State Route 109 near Old State Road is flooded and closed near this height.  Also, Lions Park ball fields will begin flooding near this height.
Near this height, floodwaters will reach the floors of the utility buildings at the wastewater treatment plant, including the generator shelter.
Highway 109 at Eureka Senior High School is closed.
The right bank begins to overflow.
Lighthouse Storage property begins to flood.  Shelters and roads within Route 66 State Park are also flooded at this level.
Numerous homes on West Spring River Ranch Road begin flooding at or slightly above this level.
Near this height, the parking lot, restrooms, and smaller pavilion along Williams Road at Kircher Park in Eureka begin flooding.
At this height, about 12 houses on Willman Road start flooding the main floors.  On the river's north side, another 8 homes along West Spring River Ranch Road begin flooding.
Near this height, the large pavilion floor and gazebo along Williams Road at Kircher Park in Eureka begin flooding.
The lowest house on West Spring River Ranch Road begins flooding on the lower level.  Also, at least one home on Willman Road begins flooding near this height.
The Route 66 State Park road to the equestrian trailhead and to the boat ramp is flooded.
Hornecker Road is closed near this height.
The athletic fields at Eureka Senior High School begin flooding near this height.
Spring River Ranch Road begins flooding near this height on the east end.

hydronotes.statement: River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 24 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.
River forecasts for this location take into account past precipitation and the precipitation amounts expected approximately 48 hours into the future from the forecast issuance time.

value.label: North American Vertical Datum of 1988
value.description: A fixed reference for elevations determined by geodetic leveling. The datum was derived from a general adjustment of the first-order terrestrial leveling nets of the United States, Canada, and Mexico. In the adjustment, only the height of the primary tidal bench mark, referenced to the International Great Lakes Datum of 1985 (IGLD 85) local mean sea level height value, at Father Point, Rimouski, Quebec, Canada was held fixed, thus providing minimum constraint. NAVD 88 and IGLD 85 are identical. However, NAVD 88 bench mark values are given in Helmert orthometric height units while IGLD 85 values are in dynamic heights. See International Great Lakes Datum of 1985, National Geodetic Vertical Datum of 1929, and geopotential difference. NAVD 88 should not be used as Mean Sea Level.

gauge.forecastReliability: Forecasts are issued as needed during times of high water, but are not routinely available.
